reason: Luzardo is elite: xERA 1.42, K% 38.8, averaging 9.3 K/start in his last 3. Recent outings: 9K, 6K, 13K — all over the line with ease. NYY vs LHP: wRC+ 70 (poor), K% 33.1 — one of the most strikeout-prone lineups in baseball right now. A 33% K rate vs a pitcher generating Ks at nearly 39% is an exceptional overlay. Adjusted expectation given NYY's elite K rate is 9+ Ks, well above the 7.5 line. The line appears set for a normal game; Luzardo is not a normal pitcher right now. At +108 this is clearly mispriced.

Skubal is the best pitcher in baseball by xERA (2.24) with a 34.3% K rate. He has gone 9K, 9K, 9K in his last 3 starts — all above the 8.5 line. KCR vs LHP: wRC+ 87 (below avg), K% 23.7 — a decent but not elite K rate. The flag notes recent opponents PHI (29%) and LAA (33%) had high K rates, and KCR at 23.7% is lower, which would argue for calibrating down slightly. However, Skubal's 34.3% K rate is so dominant that even against a below-average strikeout lineup, 9+ Ks is well within range. Adjusted expectation ~8.5-9 Ks. At +114 with 9-9-9 recent track record, this is positive expected value. KCR also faces Beck Way (NO STATS) meaning a manager may use the bullpen early on the KCR side, giving Skubal a full workload. Avg IP 5.7 and pitch counts of 96/87/85 support going deep.

Bryce Miller is elite: xERA 2.75, recent ERA 2.09, going 8K, 11K, 7K in his last 3 starts. He dominates one side of this game. Gore is struggling (recent ERA 7.94) but also at a dome (Globe Life Field) that limits weather variance. The key under signal is Miller — elite xERA (2.75 vs season ERA 3.06, no meaningful luck gap), hot recent form, and a TEX offense that is only average in L12 (wRC+ 107). SEA offense is also just average (wRC+ 106 vs LHP). Both bullpens are questionable (TEX bullpen ERA 7.31 suggests late-game blowups), but the under at 7.5 in a dome gives enough cushion that Miller's dominant first 5-6 innings keeps the early scoring low and even with Gore struggling, TEX's average offense may not pile up runs. SEA bullpen (xERA 3.60) is solid. The under is supported primarily by Miller's dominance holding SEA's half of the game in check, and TEX's offense being average. Line at 7.5 is modest and priced at -108, which is fair value.

Both starters are extremely hot right now. Trevor Rogers: recent ERA 0.50 over his last 3 starts (5IP/0ER, 6.1IP/1ER, 7IP/0ER), xERA 4.26 — ERA well below xERA signals some luck, but the recent 3-start ERA is extraordinary and at 5.8 IP/gs he goes deep. Grant Holmes: recent ERA 1.96, xERA 3.25, ERA 2.40 — ERA is below xERA here too suggesting modest luck but he's been dominant regardless. Both offenses are cold/average: ATL vs LHP wRC+ 96, BAL vs RHP wRC+ 90. BAL bullpen is elite (xERA 2.03). ATL bullpen is average (xERA 3.72) but both starters are going deep. Rogers has dominated at home (3gs, 1.99 ERA, 6.0 IP/gs) and vs ATL specifically (1gs, 0.00 ERA). Rogers' matchup history vs ATL and at home are both exceptional. Both team run totals in Rogers' L5 starts average just 3.2 RS for BAL and 3.6 RS for ATL. Total of 9.0 seems too high for this pitching matchup. Clear under signal with 3+ factors aligned.

Two elite starters. Joey Cantillo: xERA 2.44 (elite), recent 3-start ERA 1.69 (true ERA, ignoring the skewed 4 ER start which the data flags). He's averaging 6.7 K/start and going 5-6 IP regularly. Shane McClanahan: xERA 3.09 (good), recent ERA 2.37, strong at home (3gs 1.58 ERA, 5.7 IP/gs). CLE offense vs LHP is average (wRC+ 102). TBR vs LHP is elite (wRC+ 144) — the one counter-signal — but McClanahan has been holding opponents down regardless, and the Tropicana dome eliminates weather factors. Both bullpens are solid (CLE xERA 2.72, TBR xERA 3.38). Three clear under factors: both starters are performing at an elite level right now, both bullpens are good, and the dome eliminates any offensive weather factor. TBR's elite wRC+ vs LHP is the primary risk but McClanahan's recent success limits the concern.
